title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
On a beautiful evening at the Doverton villa, Mr. Satterthwaite attends a lavish tea party hosted by Tom Addison. Tom, an aging but dashing man with gouty feet and mismatched red and green slippers, welcomes his guests. Among the attendees are Air Force Captain Simon Gilliat, a handsome man with red hair, and Beryl Gilt, who manages the tea service.

Mr. Satterthwaite observes the younger generation, including two half-brothers, Roland and Timothy, who are in their mid-twenties. Although they share a collaborative spirit and red hair, Timothy possesses fair skin and a fine figure reminiscent of Mr. Satterthwaite's deceased acquaintance, Lily. The group also includes Inez, a beautiful girl of Spanish heritage and daughter of the kind, retired Dr. Horton. Inez is Rowan's first cousin and lives in the house next door. Mr. Satterthwaite notes that both young men seem to have an interest in Inez.

As the party progresses with sandwiches and colorful teacups, the conversation touches on various topics, including Timothy's nacre pipe brought from Germany. While observing the scenery of the river and the nearby mill, Mr. Satterthwaite reflects on his past connections to the house and the people present. He observes that Beryl Gilt has taken the place of Simon's first wife, Lily, and concludes that while no one physically resembles her, her presence is felt at the gathering. He also notes a perceived tension between Tom Addison and Beryl.
